title: H1 — formatMonthlyProgressEmail mock uses unknown[] for ChildStruggleLine[]
body_excerpt:
**H1 — `formatMonthlyProgressEmail` mock uses `unknown[]` for `ChildStruggleLine[]`**
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.test.ts` lines 160, 175

The real signature (`notifications.ts:387`
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/monthly-report-cron.test.ts line 202-206: formatMonthlyProgressEmail mock now explicitly uses `(to: string, body: string, _struggleLines: ChildStruggleLine[])` with the correct typed parameter. The `ChildStruggleLine` type is imported at the top of the file.

title: H2 — startSession double-cast bypasses a real type incompatibility
body_excerpt:
**H2 — `startSession` double-cast bypasses a real type incompatibility**
`apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.test.ts:184`

`startSession as unknown as typeof import('./session-crud').startSession`
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session/session-crud.test.ts lines 197-200: The cast was refactored. The pattern `as unknown as typeof import('./session-crud').startSession` no longer exists. The function now casts only the return value: `jest.fn(async () => ({ id: 'session-1' }) as unknown as LearningSession)` with a comment explaining the intentional incompatibility. The double-cast-to-function-type is gone.

title: H1: AppVariables.profileId typed string but middleware sets string | undefined
body_excerpt:
**H1: `AppVariables.profileId` typed `string` but middleware sets `string | undefined`**

`profile-scope.ts:51` explicitly declares `profileId: string | undefined`. The new `AppVariables` in `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:11`
evidence: apps/api/src/types/hono.ts line 11: `profileId: string | undefined;` — the fix was applied. Additionally, all metering-middleware-set fields are also correctly typed: `subscriptionId: string | undefined`, `subscriptionTier: SubscriptionTier | undefined`, `llmTier: LLMTier | undefined`.

title: H2: session-operations.integration.test.ts:306 — raw cast papers over a return-type lie
body_excerpt:
**H2: `session-operations.integration.test.ts:306` — raw cast papers over a return-type lie**

```ts
const firstMeta = firstResult.metadata as Record<string, unknown>;
```

`syncHomeworkState` is declared to return
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session-operations.integration.test.ts line 306: `firstResult.metadata.loggedCorrectionIds` is now accessed directly without the `as Record<string, unknown>` cast. The raw cast is gone; the test accesses typed fields directly on the result.

title: M1 — BASE_ENV: Env fixture uses post-coercion numeric literals
body_excerpt:
**M1 — `BASE_ENV: Env` fixture uses post-coercion numeric literals**
`apps/api/src/config.test.ts:28-30`

`MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_THRESHOLD: 0.15`, `MAX_DEDUP_LLM_CALLS_PER_SESSION: 10`, and `MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_ROLLOUT_PCT: 0`
evidence: apps/api/src/config.test.ts lines 29-31: `MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_THRESHOLD: 0.15`, `MAX_DEDUP_LLM_CALLS_PER_SESSION: 10`, `MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_ROLLOUT_PCT: 0` remain as JS numeric literals. No new test exercises `validateEnv` with raw string inputs to cover the coercion path.
suggested_notion_title: config.test.ts BASE_ENV uses post-coercion numbers, no string coercion coverage
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/config.test.ts lines 29-31 use JS numeric literals for Zod-coerced env vars. The raw string→number coercion path is untested. Add a test that pipes a `Record<string, string>` through `validateEnv` to exercise `z.coerce.number()`. Low-risk but leaves a gap in env validation coverage.

title: M2 — PromiseLike.then reassignment is fragile (pre-existing)
body_excerpt:
**M2 — `PromiseLike.then` reassignment is fragile (pre-existing)**
`apps/api/src/services/book-suggestion-generation.test.ts:112`

`PromiseLike<unknown[]>` is technically more correct than `Promise<unknown[]>`
evidence: apps/api/src/services/book-suggestion-generation.test.ts line 111-119: The code was refactored. The old PromiseLike.then reassignment is gone; the mock chain now uses jest.fn<() => Promise<unknown[]>>().mockResolvedValue(result), which is not fragile. The CCR noted this was pre-existing and no action needed beyond noting it; the refactor improved it anyway.

title: M3 — event.name removal in review-calibration-grade.test.ts is correct
body_excerpt:
**M3 — `event.name` removal in `review-calibration-grade.test.ts` is correct**
`apps/api/src/inngest/functions/review-calibration-grade.test.ts:8`

This is correct — no action needed
evidence: CCR itself stated no action needed. This was an informational note only.

title: M1: topup-expiry-reminder.test.ts:201,245 — (args.length as number) cast is a no-op
body_excerpt:
**M1: `topup-expiry-reminder.test.ts:201,245` — `(args.length as number)` cast is a no-op**

`ConstructorParameters<typeof Date>` is a rest-parameter tuple. In TS strict mode, comparing `.length` to `0` triggers TS2367
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/topup-expiry-reminder.test.ts lines 199 and 248: Both use `if (!args.length)` (the suggested fix) instead of `(args.length as number)`. The no-op cast is gone.

title: M2: export.test.ts:316,325 — non-null assertions on optional field hide future failures
body_excerpt:
**M2: `export.test.ts:316,325` — non-null assertions on optional field hide future failures**

`result.sessionEvents!.find(...)` assumes the mock always populates the optional field.
evidence: apps/api/src/services/export.test.ts lines 316, 322, 331: `expect(result.sessionEvents).toBeDefined()` was added before the access, `expect(aiRow).toBeDefined()` and `expect(userRow).toBeDefined()` guard the subsequent `!` accesses. The guard assertions are in place.

title: M3: session-operations.integration.test.ts — non-narrowing assertion before type cast
body_excerpt:
**M3: `session-operations.integration.test.ts` — non-narrowing assertion before type cast**

`expect(transcript !== null && !transcript.archived).toBe(true)` does not narrow the TS type
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session-operations.integration.test.ts lines 165-168: Pattern changed to the throw form: `if (!transcript || transcript.archived) { throw new Error(...) }` — matching the pattern already used elsewhere in the file, as suggested.

title: File: apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:13 — subscriptionId and llmTier should be string | undefined
body_excerpt:
**File**: `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:13`

`profileId` was correctly fixed to `string | undefined` (CR-1) because the middleware only sets it when a profile resolves. `subscriptionId` has the identical problem
evidence: apps/api/src/types/hono.ts lines 13-16: `subscriptionId: string | undefined`, `subscriptionTier: SubscriptionTier | undefined`, `llmTier: LLMTier | undefined` — all metering-middleware-set fields are typed correctly with `| undefined`.

title: GC1 exemptions need stronger justification in weekly-self-reports.test.ts and weekly-progress-push.test.ts
body_excerpt: **GC1 exemptions need stronger justification.** `weekly-self-reports.test.ts` and `weekly-progress-push.test.ts` each introduce 6-7 new `jest.mock` calls on internal relative-path modules, all tagged `gc1-allow: unit test boundary`
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/weekly-progress-push.test.ts lines 90, 118, 130, 167: still annotated `gc1-allow: unit test boundary` (generic reason). weekly-self-reports.test.ts uses `gc1-allow: pattern-a conversion` on all mocks but services like solo-progress-reports, snapshot-aggregation, weekly-report remain internally mocked. The CCR finding called for either converting to integration tests or spelling out why integration is infeasible — neither was done. The justification remains generic.
suggested_notion_title: GC1 exemptions in weekly cron tests still have weak justifications
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/weekly-progress-push.test.ts (lines 90, 118, 130, 167) and weekly-self-reports.test.ts still mock core orchestrated services (snapshot-aggregation, weekly-report, solo-progress-reports, settings) with generic `gc1-allow: unit test boundary` or `pattern-a conversion` justifications. The CCR required either converting to integration tests or explaining why integration is infeasible. Neither was done. Action: either add explicit rationale why the Inngest step runner prevents integration testing, or convert to integration tests.

title: Unvalidated subjectId in dictation and quiz routes
body_excerpt:
**Unvalidated `subjectId` in dictation and quiz routes**
See inline on `packages/schemas/src/dictation.ts:76`. The `subjectId` field accepted in `POST /v1/dictation/results` (and for non-vocabulary quiz rounds
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/dictation.ts line 165: `subjectId: input.subjectId ?? null` is passed to `recordDictationResult` without ownership check. apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts lines 45-66: `subjectId` goes directly into `recordPracticeActivityEvent` without verifying it belongs to `profileId`. apps/api/src/services/quiz/orchestrate-round.ts line 93: non-vocabulary path passes `input.subjectId ?? null` to `generateQuizRound` without ownership check. Only the vocabulary path uses `createScopedRepository` (via `getVocabularyRoundContext`). The FK can point to another profile's subject row.
suggested_notion_title: SECURITY: unvalidated subjectId in dictation results and non-vocabulary quiz rounds
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/routes/dictation.ts and apps/api/src/services/quiz/orchestrate-round.ts (line 93) accept a caller-supplied subjectId and store it in practice_activity_events without verifying the subject belongs to the caller's profile. The vocabulary path validates via createScopedRepository but the non-vocabulary path does not. A client can submit another profile's subjectId — the FK will point to a foreign row. Add a scoped ownership check (repo.subjects.findFirst) before writing the event in both paths. Matches CLAUDE.md: 'Writes must include explicit profileId protection or verify ownership through the parent chain.'

title: File: apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts line 29 — PgTransaction double-cast
body_excerpt:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 29

Drizzle's `PgTransaction` has a narrower surface than `NodePgDatabase`. The double cast erases the type mismatch at compile time.
evidence: apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts line 31: `const txDb = tx as unknown as Database;` — the double-cast is still present with no explanatory comment. The CCR asked for a comment linking to a tracked issue if the refactor is out of scope. No such comment exists.
suggested_notion_title: dictation/result.ts PgTransaction double-cast lacks rationale comment
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts line 31: `tx as unknown as Database` still present, no comment linking to a tracked issue. The CCR asked for either the refactor or a comment. Same pattern exists in quiz/complete-round.ts. Low-severity but the cast silently papers over a type mismatch. Add a comment or link to a tracked issue.

title: File: apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts line 45 — localDate appended as UTC midnight
body_excerpt:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 45

`localDate` is a calendar date in the learner's local timezone. Appending `T00:00:00.000Z` pins it to UTC midnight
evidence: apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts line 29: `const completedAt = new Date();` — the service now uses the server timestamp at time of call rather than constructing a date from localDate + T00:00:00.000Z. The UTC-midnight misrepresentation is gone.

title: File: apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts line 32 — colon-collision dedupeKey format
body_excerpt:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts` line 32

```ts
return `${activityType}:${sourceType}${subtype}:${sourceId}${occurrence}`;
```

When `activitySubtype` is present the subtype is spliced
evidence: apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts lines 30-40: `buildPracticeActivityDedupeKey` now uses `encodeURIComponent` for every segment via `encodeSegment`/`encodeOptionalSegment`, with `|` as the separator and explicit sentinels for null optional values. The fragile template-literal format is gone.

title: File: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts line 102 — /[?!.:]/ regex causes false-positive homework cue detection
body_excerpt:
**File:** `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts` line 102

`/[?!.:]/.test(text)` returns `true` for any period, which matches abbreviations (`Dr.`), URLs, and ordinary prose.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts line 107: pattern is now `/[?!:]/` — the period (`.`) has been removed from the character class. Only `?`, `!`, and `:` are matched, reducing false-positive suppression from period-containing text.

title: Colon-collision in buildCelebrationDedupeKey — celebration-events.ts:25
body_excerpt:
**1. Colon-collision in `buildCelebrationDedupeKey`** — see inline on `celebration-events.ts:25`
The separator character `:` is not encoded, so distinct `(celebrationType, reason, sourceId)` tuples can produce the same key
evidence: apps/api/src/services/celebration-events.ts lines 25-31: `buildCelebrationDedupeKey` now uses `encodeURIComponent` on every segment (`encodeSegment`) before joining with `:`. Colon-collision for values containing `:` is prevented.

title: Telemetry write inside transaction with no error isolation — assessments.ts:172-178
body_excerpt:
**2. Telemetry write inside transaction with no error isolation** — see inline on `assessments.ts:172–178`
`recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` is called inside the Drizzle transaction.
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/assessments.ts lines 200-218: `recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` is now called OUTSIDE the `db.transaction()` block (transaction ends at line 199), wrapped in its own try/catch with `captureException`. Activity event recording cannot roll back the core assessment update.

title: practice_activity_events.source_type has no DB-level constraint; OCR buffers full body before size check; NOT VALID lacks rationale; inconsistent dedupeKey format at recitation/fluency_drill
body_excerpt: **`practice_activity_events.source_type` has no DB-level constraint.** Unlike `celebration_events`, which gets the `source_type_known` CHECK constraint in migration 0074, `practice_activity_events.source_type` is unconstrained text.
evidence: Multiple sub-issues: (1) No migration after 0074 adds a CHECK constraint to practice_activity_events.source_type — grep of drizzle/0075-0077 shows no match. (2) apps/api/src/routes/homework.ts lines 57-82: `c.req.parseBody()` is called before `file.size` check; no body-size middleware or wrangler.toml limit found. (3) 0074 migration has no rationale comment for NOT VALID. (4) apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts lines 1885-1888: recitation uses hand-rolled `joinDedupeKey(['recitation','session_event',encodeDedupeSegment(aiEventId)], ':')` while fluency_drill (line 1897-1911) omits explicit dedupeKey and relies on auto-generated format.
suggested_notion_title: PR-241 bundle: source_type no CHECK constraint; OCR pre-body-parse size check; recitation/fluency_drill dedupeKey inconsistency
suggested_notion_body: Four open issues from PR-241 CCR: (1) practice_activity_events.source_type has no DB CHECK constraint; a typo silently inserts an invalid value — add a CHECK constraint or at minimum a shared constant. (2) apps/api/src/routes/homework.ts: full multipart body is parsed before the size check — enforce limit via Hono body-size middleware or wrangler.toml `limits.fetch_body_size_limit`. (3) 0074 migration uses NOT VALID with no SQL comment explaining why — add one. (4) session-exchange.ts recitation uses a hand-rolled dedupeKey format while fluency_drill uses auto-generated format from buildPracticeActivityDedupeKey — standardize both on one approach.

title: Silent practice-event drop has no queryable failure rate; OCR error strings not i18n'd; migration 0074 missing rollback section
body_excerpt:
**Silent practice-event drop has no queryable failure rate** (inline on `apps/api/src/routes/assessments.ts:174`)
The `try/catch` around `recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` fires `captureException` but Sentry exceptions are sampled.
evidence: (1) apps/api/src/routes/assessments.ts lines 200-218: try/catch fires `captureException` only — no tagged metric or Inngest retry. Sentry is sampled; failure rate is not queryable. (2) apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts line 404: `"We couldn't read that clearly. Try taking the photo again with better lighting."` is a hardcoded English string — not found in any i18n locale file. (3) apps/api/drizzle/0074_celebration_events_source_type_check.sql has no rollback section or comments. CLAUDE.md requires a Rollback section for migrations altering existing tables.
suggested_notion_title: PR-241 bundle: silent telemetry drop, hardcoded OCR i18n string, missing migration rollback doc
suggested_notion_body: Three open issues: (1) assessments.ts try/catch uses only captureException for recordAssessmentCompletionActivity failures — add a tagged metric or safeSend Inngest event so failure rate is queryable per CLAUDE.md rule. (2) use-homework-ocr.ts line 404: hardcoded English error string 'We couldn't read that clearly...' not in i18n locale files — must go through translation system like sibling NON_HOMEWORK_ERROR_MESSAGE keys. (3) 0074 migration adds NOT VALID constraint with no rollback section in any plan doc — CLAUDE.md requires this for migrations altering existing tables.
